assertion_results.text: Step 0 extracts the Embargo policy URL from the Security Configuration as an optional field without raising an error (§1.71 — backward compatible extraction)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md Configuration section shows: 'Embargo policy URL is configured in Security Configuration: Embargo policy URL: https://example.com/security/embargo-policy' and 'Step 1.7 is not skipped because the Embargo policy URL is present.' The phrasing 'is not skipped because ... is present' demonstrates the field is treated as optional (would be skipped if absent). No error is raised. The URL was extracted from Security Configuration and used downstream without issue.

assertion_results.text: Step 1.7 evaluates the CVE severity — CVSS 7.5 (High) meets the Critical/Important threshold (CVSS >= 7.0) and triggers the embargo warning gate (§1.70)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md Severity Evaluation table shows: CVSS score 7.5, Severity High, Embargo threshold CVSS >= 7.0 (Critical or Important), Threshold met? YES -- 7.5 >= 7.0. Explicit statement: 'The CVSS score of 7.5 (High severity) meets the embargo trigger threshold of >= 7.0. The warning gate is triggered.'

assertion_results.text: Step 1.7 presents a warning gate to the engineer including the CVE ID (CVE-2026-31812), severity level, a statement about embargo risk, and a link to the configured embargo policy URL (https://example.com/security/embargo-policy) (§1.70)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md Warning Gate section presents a formatted gate with all four required elements: (1) CVE ID: 'CVE-2026-31812' in the header, (2) severity level: 'High severity', (3) embargo risk statement: 'High-severity vulnerabilities may be under embargo. Before proceeding, verify with your security team that this CVE is cleared for public triage.', (4) embargo policy link: 'Embargo policy: https://example.com/security/embargo-policy'.

assertion_results.text: Step 1.7 requires explicit engineer confirmation (Yes/No) before proceeding to Step 2 — the gate is a confirmation prompt, not an informational message (§1.70)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md Warning Gate ends with 'Proceed with triage? (Yes / No)' — an explicit confirmation prompt. Gate Behavior section confirms: 'If the engineer answers Yes: proceed to Step 2 (Version Impact Analysis). If the engineer answers No: stop execution immediately.' Decision Logic Summary item 4 reiterates: 'The gate requires explicit Yes/No confirmation before the skill proceeds to Step 2.' This is a blocking confirmation gate, not informational.

assertion_results.text: The embargo warning gate does NOT trigger for Low or Moderate severity CVEs (CVSS < 7.0) — it is skipped silently when severity is below threshold (§1.70)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md explicitly states at the end: 'Note: If the CVSS were below 7.0 (Low or Moderate severity), this step would be skipped silently and triage would proceed directly to Step 2 without any embargo warning.' The threshold is defined as 'CVSS >= 7.0' in the Severity Evaluation table, confirming values below 7.0 do not trigger the gate.
